design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore domain → collect golden artifact → return to Animal King → unlock second half → find elemental spell → defeat boss → obtain IronSword piece → repeat across four domains → final battle
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (magic, health, money) balanced against exploration and puzzle-solving to unlock boss encounters; mandatory spell collection creates gating mechanism
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Knight warrior Kuros assembling legendary IronSword by traversing elemental domains and defeating mythologically-themed bosses; progression toward confronting evil wizard Malkil
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces spell-gating for boss encounters—bosses are unkillable and room is impassable without domain-specific elemental spells, removing player choice in approach; strict structural progression (Earth/Wind/Fire/Water domains) with no sequence breaking; heavy reliance on item collection and resource economy rather than pure platforming skill

difficulty.lives_system: Life meter that depletes from enemy damage, projectiles, and fall damage; death occurs when meter empties; limited extra lives available through item collection
difficulty.continues_system: Players can continue up to two times, restarting where they left off; continue function disabled after Earth domain (or immediately upon entry due to documented bug)
difficulty.checkpointing: Password feature allows saving progress and continuing after console reset; no in-level checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Low—difficulty is fixed; game offers consumable defensive spells (invincibility via Silver Fleece) and healing items (chicken, brew) to mitigate challenge, but no adjustable difficulty settings; puzzle-gating (finding spells) is mandatory, not optional

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon and armor upgrades purchasable with collected money; magic spells (both utility and boss-specific) found or purchased; shields, helmets, and keys obtained through treasure chests and shops
progression.unlock_mechanism: Golden artifacts unlock second half of each domain; elemental spells mandatory to damage and survive boss encounters; keys unlock treasure chests; progression locked to four sequential elemental domains
replayability_hooks: Hall of Fame score tracking (resets on console power-off)
Relics of Sindarin collection (book, cross, gauntlet, ring) for point scoring
Bonus chance gambling minigame in inns
Multiple spell effects encourage experimentation (Familiar Spell converts enemies to money, Dragon Tooth to food, etc.)

world_building_techniques: Greek elemental theming (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water domains with corresponding Elemental bosses)
Artifact collection narrative (Animal Kings and golden artifacts)
Destination framing (quest to IceFire Mountain to defeat Malkil)
Environmental structure (four domains as distinct mythological spaces)
In-game inn NPCs and shop interactions (implied social world)

boss_design.philosophy: Screen-filling designs using minimal moving sprites; designed to be visually impressive while respecting NES hardware constraints; mandatory spell-gating ensures focused encounters—bosses cannot be damaged without correct elemental spell, removing player improvisation
notable_bosses: Wind Elemental (first boss, demonstrates screen-filling design)
Malkil (final boss at IceFire Mountain, requires assembled IronSword)

faction_indicators: Elemental domains as distinct territories (Earth/Wind/Fire/Water ruled by Animal Kings)
Malkil as central antagonist unifying all four domains
danger_signals: Spell-gating creates vulnerability—entering boss room without correct spell = game-over situation
Fall damage and enemy projectiles signal environmental hazards
environmental_storytelling: Domain theming (elemental properties reflected in level design and boss forms)
Animal King presence in each domain (implies hierarchy and protection)
Treasure chest distribution (encourages exploration and resource discovery)
Inn locations and shop economies (imply inhabited civilizations within domains)
